temperature.note: Stable, trending down slightly. Within target range 72-78°F.

ph.note: Stable near target midpoint. Creeping up slightly (+0.04 in 24h), which is normal during cycling.

tds.note: Comfortably centered in target range. Slight rise (+4ppm in 24h) consistent with bioload accumulation during cycle.

reasoning: Day 15 shows healthy chemistry across all three parameters, but the nitrogen cycle remains incomplete: ammonia still 0.25ppm (should be <0.1 before shrimp intro), nitrite still 5ppm (still toxic—target <0.5ppm), nitrate stable at 160ppm (good—bacteria are working). The system is progressing but not yet safe for shrimp.
recommended_actions: Continue daily ammonia and nitrite testing. Watch for ammonia to drop below 0.1ppm.
Nitrite at 5ppm is still concerning—track its decline. Full cycle completion typically sees both ammonia and nitrite spike, then crash simultaneously.
Hold off on water changes unless TDS creeps above 200ppm. The bioload and nutrients are fueling the cycle.
Temperature and pH are behaving well. No intervention needed there.
suggested_actuator_actions: No actuator intervention recommended. Passive cycling in progress.

temperature.note: Stable, within range. 24hr mean 76.14°F, slight cooling trend (-1.01°F). Excellent for cycle.

ph.note: Acidifying. 24hr mean 6.527, floor at 6.38. Approaching watch threshold (6.45). Monitor buffer capacity.

tds.note: Ideal range. Stable around 168ppm mean. Organic waste accumulating slowly—normal for cycle phase.

reasoning: Ammonia holding steady at 0.25ppm with strong nitrite conversion (80→5ppm in 24hrs suggests ~3x faster nitrification), indicating Nitrosomonas maturity. However, nitrite plateau at 2-5ppm and pH drift downward signal Nitrobacter lag and acid buildup—classic mid-cycle squeeze. Day 15 timeline still viable if pH stabilizes above 6.45.
recommended_actions: Monitor pH floor over next 48hrs; if drops below 6.45, prepare light buffer (crushed coral or baking soda test).
Continue daily ammonia/nitrite/nitrate tracking—completion window is day 20-22.
Observe for ANY ammonia spike (>0.5ppm sustained) or pH collapse below 6.3; these signal bacterial stress.
No water change yet—preserving Nitrobacter culture and organic load needed for cycle pressure.
suggested_actuator_actions: Hold all actuators. Manual observation mode optimal for cycling tank.
If pH hits 6.40, prepare to dose 0.5tsp baking soda (experimental—Toby approval required).
Temperature stable; heater/cooler remain standby only.

temperature.note: Stable 24h mean 76.13°F, slight cooling trend (-0.89°F). Well within target 72-78°F.

ph.note: Drifting toward lower end of target (6.5-7.5). Watch for further acidification; currently +0.02 over 24h but floor risk at 6.45.

tds.note: Mid-range, +7ppm over 24h accumulation. Normal for cycling bioload.

reasoning: Ammonia holding steady at 0.25ppm with robust consumption; pH now at edge of acceptable range and bears close watch. Nitrite spike to 5ppm then plateau at 2ppm suggests Nitrobacter lag—cycle progressing but still day 15 of ~20, system is stressed but resilient.
recommended_actions: Continue daily ammonia/nitrite/nitrate tests; target cycle completion by day 20-22.
Monitor pH closely—if it dips below 6.45 or shows further decline, consider 25% water change with RO or small pH buffer dose.
Observe bacterial behavior over next 48h: Nitrobacter conversion rate will determine cycle timeline.
suggested_actuator_actions: No immediate intervention needed; hold temperature at current stable level (75°F ideal).
Stand by for pH correction if next test shows <6.45; do not act preemptively.

temperature.note: Stable, slightly cooling trend. Within ideal range.

ph.note: Low side of target but holding. Upward 24hr trend is good; watch floor at 6.45.

tds.note: Healthy mid-range. Slight rise reflects bioload accumulation during cycle.

reasoning: Ammonia and nitrite remain elevated (0.25ppm and 2ppm respectively), but the ammonia hold steady and nitrate jumped sharply from 80→160ppm in 24h, suggesting Nitrosomonas are aggressive while Nitrobacter lag—classic mid-cycle asymmetry. pH drifting acidic as expected from nitrification; still within bacteria tolerance but needs watching.
recommended_actions: Continue daily testing through day 20 to track nitrite decline—this is your cycle completion signal.
If pH drops below 6.45, prepare small water change (10-15%) to stabilize; bacteria can handle 6.5+.
Monitor ammonia: stable 0.25ppm is acceptable for cycle phase; spike above 0.5ppm warrants intervention.
suggested_actuator_actions: Hold all actuator changes—parameters are stable and bacteria are working.
Prepare heater/cooler standby; temp trending down slightly but still safe.

temperature.note: Stable, within target. 24hr mean 76.1°F, slight cooling trend (-0.45°F).

ph.note: Lower bound approaching. 24hr mean 6.53, creeping down. Watch for further acidification.

tds.note: Mid-range, healthy. +5ppm rise in 24hr likely from biofilm buildup during cycle.

reasoning: Nitrite plateau at 2ppm (down from 5ppm yesterday) is encouraging—Nitrobacter may be accelerating—but ammonia holding steady at 0.25ppm and pH drifting lower signal a system working hard under acid load. We're on the knife's edge of cycle completion; bacterial metabolism is outpacing buffering capacity.
recommended_actions: Continue daily testing (ammonia, nitrite, nitrate) through day 18-20 to catch cycle completion window.
If pH drops below 6.45 or ammonia spikes, perform 25-30% water change immediately.
Monitor nitrite trend closely—decline to <1ppm within next 48-72hr would indicate Nitrobacter dominance.
No intervention needed today; system stable but watch list items remain active.
suggested_actuator_actions: None at this time. Manual monitoring sufficient.
Prepare water change protocol if pH hits 6.45 or ammonia >0.5ppm.

temperature.note: Stable mid-range, holding well. -0.23°F decline over 24h is normal drift.

ph.note: Acidifying trend. Now 0.07 below target floor (6.5). Approaching watch threshold of 6.45.

tds.note: Healthy bioload accumulation. +5ppm/24h rise is expected during cycling.

reasoning: Ammonia and nitrite remain elevated but stable (0.25ppm and 2ppm respectively), indicating robust bacterial consumption; however, pH is drifting acidic and now breaches the lower target boundary, which could slow Nitrobacter if it continues. Day 15 shows good cycle progression, but the pH trend merits close observation—one more drop and we escalate to buffer consideration.
recommended_actions: Test again in 12 hours; flag immediately if pH falls below 6.45
Monitor ammonia and nitrite trend—expect both to decline toward completion by day 20-22
Observe bacterial behavior; no action needed yet, but acidification can slow Nitrobacter oxidation if severe
Consider small pH buffer dose (calcium carbonate) only if pH hits 6.40 or lower
suggested_actuator_actions: No actuator intervention required at this time
Temperature hold: heater/chiller remain on standby—current 76.21°F is stable and optimal
